title: trema molle
intro: Trema molle, also known as the White Teak or Batai tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae. Native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Trema molle include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Trema molle can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or topically to harness its therapeutic benefits.
